sglang.title: [Feature] Support reward model LxzGordon/URM-LLaMa-3.1-8B
sglang.author: Ying1123
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds support for the reward model LxzGordon/URM-LLaMa-3.1-8B. It currently supports SGLang native REST API of http://{host}:{port}/judge.
Example usage:
# launch server
python -m sglang.launch_server --model LxzGordon/URM-LLaMa-3.1-8B --is-embedding

# send requests
import json

import reque

vllm.title: [Kernel] AQ AZP 4/4: Integrate asymmetric quantization to linear method
vllm.author: ProExpertProg
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Add end-to-end support for (dynamic) asymmetric activation quantization by invoking AZP quant and scaled_mm kernels in linear method. Both dynamic asymmetric AQ and static asymmetric AQ implemented.

vllm.title: [Core] Multi-Step + Single Step Prefills via Chunked Prefill code path
vllm.author: varun-sundar-rabindranath
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Adds support for scheduling prompts with decodes in Multi-Step. This PR uses the Chunked-Prefill code path to this effect.
Adding chunked-prompts to multi-step, in realization of a full chunked-prefill support, is a future-work and needs some investigation as it is not clear that it will have a posi

vllm.title: [misc][distributed] add VLLM_SKIP_P2P_CHECK flag
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
if users can trust their driver, they can opt in to skip the check (which takes ~30 seconds for tp=8)
note that one cluster usually has one driver version, so it is feasible for users to test it in one node, and then decide if they want to trust the driver directly.

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Intel] Fix XPU Dockerfile Build
vllm.author: tylertitsworth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The XPU Dockerfile consistently wasn't building, I've worked out some of the issues and assigned the correct IPEX XPU versions to be installed in the image.
I also added support for the openai server version of xpu. I validated on a Max 1100.
Because Ubuntu 20.04 isn't supported by the Triton XPU Ba
